AFTER RADAR ANALYSIS AND A STORM SURVEY...THIS WAS DETERMINED TO BE A LAND SPOUT TORNADO. TWO 75 FOOT TALL HAM RADIO TOWERS BLOWN DOWN WITH ONE FALLING ON ROOF OF RESIDENCE. FENCE WITH CONCRETE POSTS IN GROUND BLOWN OVER.

AFTER A STORM SURVEY AND RADAR ANALYSIS...THIS WAS DETERMINED TO BE A LAND SPOUT TORNADO. THE TORNADO CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE AT TECH OIL ON THE NORTHEAST SIDE OF ACADIANA REGIONAL AIRPORT. OVERHEAD DOOR BLOWN OUT AND A CARPORT WAS DAMAGED. FOUR CARS WERE DAMAGED WHEN THE CARPORT FELL. THE INJURY PREVIOUSLY REPORTED WAS NOT DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE TORNADO ITSELF.

TSTM WIND DAMAGE UPGRADED TO TORNADO BASED ON NWS STORM SURVEY. DAMAGE FOUND FROM EF1 TORNADO ALONG A PATH FROM 2 MILES SOUTH OF PALMETTO TO 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF PALMETTO. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN OR UPROOTED WITH RESIDENTS TRAPPED IN ONE HOME. SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE KNOCKED DOWN. MANY HOMES HAD ROOF DAMAGE AND ONE TIED-DOWN MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED SEVERAL TIMES AND DESTROYED. SHEDS WERE DESTROYED AND A CAMPER WAS ROLLED AND DAMAGED. NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO...ONE WHICH CONTINUED FROM 2 MILES SOUTHEAST CHATAIGNIER IN EVANGELINE PARISH...ACROSS WESTERN ST. LANDRY PARISH 1 MILE SOUTH OF PRAIRIE RONDE. AMONG THE DAMAGE FOUND WERE TIN AWINGS AND PART OF A TIN ROOF RIPPED OFF A COUPLE OF MOBILE HOMES ALONG PRAIRIE RONDE HIGHWAY/SR 103. DAMAGE TO TREE TOPS WERE OBSERVED ABOUT 1 MILE EAST OF PRAIRIE RONDE. TOTAL TRACK LENGTH OF TORNADO IN ST. LANDRY PARISH WAS 5 MILES...WITH A WIDTH OF 40 YARDS.
TREES DOWN...TIN SHEDS BLOWN APART AND POWER LINES DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.

NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO WHICH BEGAN 2 SOUTHEAST CHATAIGNIER ON ST. JUILAN ROAD/SR 95 AND LANSE BOURBEUSE ROAD/PR 1-115 IN EVANGELINE PARISH...WHERE THE GREATEST DAMAGE OCCURRED. THE TRACK ENDED 1 E OF PRAIRIE ROND IN ST. LANDRY PARISH...FOR A TOTAL LENGTH OF 8.5 MILES AND 50 YARDS WIDE. AMONG THE DAMAGE FOUND WERE NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND TREE TOPS RIPPED OFF...TIN SHEDS BLOWN APART...METAL AND SHINGLE ROOFING BLOWN OFF HOMES.

CORRECTED DATE FROM 04/10 TO 4/11. NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO...ONE WHICH CONTINUED FROM 2 MILES SOUTHEAST CHATAIGNIER IN EVANGELINE PARISH...ACROSS WESTERN ST. LANDRY PARISH 1 MILE SOUTH OF PRAIRIE RONDE. AMONG THE DAMAGE FOUND WERE TIN AWINGS AND PART OF A TIN ROOF RIPPED OFF A COUPLE OF MOBILE HOMES ALONG PRAIRIE RONDE HIGHWAY/SR 103. DAMAGE TO TREE TOPS WERE OBSERVED ABOUT 1 MILE EAST OF PRAIRIE RONDE. TOTAL TRACK LENGTH OF TORNADO IN ST. LANDRY PARISH WAS 5 MILES...WITH A WIDTH OF 40

NWS SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS CAUSED THE EXTENSIVE TREE AND POWERLINE DAMAGE ABOUT 500 YARDS WIDE ALONG SR 182 JUST NORTH OF NUBA. THIS PLACEMENT OF THE DAMAGING WINDS IS LIKELY THE REAR FLANK DOWNDRAFT PORTION OF THE THUNDERSTORM THAT PRODUCED THE TORNADO JUST A MILE SOUTH OF THIS LOCATION.

NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO WHICH BEGAN 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF OPELOUSAS AND ENDED 5 MILES NORTH OF OPELOUSAS...OR JUST SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF US 167 AND LA 182. TOTAL TRACK LENGTH WAS AROUND 4 MILES...AND TRACK WIDTH UNDETERMINED. AMONG THE DAMAGE WAS SEVERAL LARGE TREES BLOWN OVER...LARGE TREE BRANCHES SNAPPED...AND PORCH AND SHED DESTROYED ALONG DEPRIMO LANE/PR 5-20.

A TORNADO BEGAN SW OF HWY 92/HWY 343 INTERSECTION AND MOVED NORTHEAST. MOBILE HOME ON BLOCKS DESTROYED AND HOUSE MOVED OFF FOUNDATION NEAR START OF PATH. MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES IN ADOLA SUBDIVISION. SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES BLOWN DOWN. ONLY EF0 DAMAGE NOTED IN VERMILION PARISH. TORNADO CONTINUED INTO LAFAYETTE PARISH.

THIS IS A CONTINUATION OF THE TORNADO THAT BEGAN WEST OF MAURICE IN VERMILION PARISH. TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES AND TREES NOTED. THE TORNADO STRENGTHENED AS IT APPROACHED HWY 724/BREAUX RD CAUSING EF1/LOW-END EF2 DAMAGE. ONE HOME HAD ITS ROOF COMPLETELY BLOWN OFF WITH OTHER HOMES AND BUILDINGS DAMAGED. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. THE TORNADO LIFTED 1 MILE WEST OF THE MALL OF ACADIANA AT 630 AM. TOTAL PATH LENGTH 9 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 200 YARDS.

EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF AMBASSADOR CAFFERY PKWY/SETTLERS TRACE BLVD INTERSECTION IN LAFAYETTE AND MOVED NORTHEAST THROUGH THE BEAULLIEU PLACE AND RIVER RANCH SUBDIVISIONS. EF0 TORNADO BEGAN IN THE THEALL COMMUNITY AND MOVED EAST CROSSING THE HWY 82/HWY 690 INTERSECTION. TORNADO THEN MOVED EAST ALONG HWY 690 AND STRUCK THE GRAND ISLE SHIPYARD NORTH OF THE BANCKER COMMUNITY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND DAMAGED ALONG THE PATH...WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES AND THE SHIPYARD. PATH LENGTH 7 MILES AND PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS.
MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE CITY. PARTS OF TIN ROOFS BLOWN OFF OF BUILDINGS.

EF1 TORNADO BEGAN SW OF COLLEGE RD/BENDEL RD INTERSECTION AND MOVED NORTHEAST ACROSS OIL CENTER AREA OF LAFAYETTE. SEVERAL BUILDINGS HAD SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE WITH DEBRIS FROM ROOFS KNOCKING OUT WINDOWS IN NEIGHBORING BUILDINGS. TREES AND POWER POLES WERE DOWNED WITH SOME TREES FALLING ON CARS. TORNADO LIFTED NE OF PINHOOK RD/UNIVERSITY AVE INTERSECTION. PATH LENGTH OF 1 MILE AND PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS.
